flags.concern: Timeline top-drop: the plan does not implement the user's existing-clip case, so dragging an existing clip to the top edge would still create the new track at the bottom.
flags.evidence: The idea explicitly includes existing clips. In the repo, internal clip drag uses `dropPosition.isNewTrack` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:367-372`, and that path calls `createTrackAndMoveClip()`. `createTrackAndMoveClip()` appends via `addTrack(current.resolvedConfig, kind)` with no insertion index or top/bottom parameter in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:145-158`. The plan only restores external-drop files and treats internal clip dragging as separate.

flags.concern: Timeline indicator plumbing: Step 3 understates the problem; restoring `TimelineCanvas` alone is not enough because the indicator path currently drops the top-vs-bottom state before it reaches the UI.
flags.evidence: `DropIndicatorPosition` does not define `trackId` or any top/bottom field in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:23-27`, while `DropIndicator` already reads `position?.trackId` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:68-77`. Upstream, `toIndicatorPosition()` only forwards `isNewTrack` and `newTrackKind` from `DropPosition` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:64-80`. So the plan's 'restore TimelineCanvas and keep the string match' approach misses the actual repo issue: the top-state is not plumbed through the coordinator/indicator path.

flags.concern: Timeline top-drop layout: the proposed top drop zone rendering is likely to destabilize hit-testing because it inserts new layout above the rows without adjusting row coordinate math.
flags.evidence: The HEAD implementation the plan wants to restore from commit `f93683867` inserts a normal-flow drop-zone `<div>` above `<DndContext>` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`, i.e. inside the scroll content before the track rows. But `computeDropPosition()` still calculates row indices and `rowTop` from `editRect.top` with no extra top offset in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:97-110`. By inspection, once that bar renders it will push the first row down while drag hit-testing still assumes row 0 starts at the old origin.
